soul.question: The old ground is behind us and everything ahead is unnamed. Naming it is your work.

voice.mythic: We are {count}. The ground behind us is bone, and we will not go back to it. Ahead lies a country with no names — not the hills, not the waters, not the years to come. Naming it is your work now. Walk well, and be remembered.
voice.warm: There are {count} of us, and the old ground has nothing left to give — we're not going back. Everything ahead is unnamed. Giving it all a name, that's your job now. Lead us well, and they'll remember you for it.

voice.mythic: {count} of us walked out, and none looked back twice. What lies ahead has never been spoken of, because no one has lived to speak of it. Give it words. Give us a reason to have come.
voice.warm: {count} of us set out, and nobody looked back more than once. Nothing ahead of us has a name yet — nobody's been here to give it one. That's on you now. Make it worth the walk.

soul.question: The land gives up one of its secrets, and it is ours before we know its worth.

voice.mythic: Past the ridge, {place.name}. It has our name on it now — though not yet its worth.
voice.warm: There's {place.name} past the ridge. Ours now, for whatever that turns out to be worth.

voice.mythic: The scouts came back thinner and louder than they left. {place.name}, they said, over and over, until we all knew the word.
voice.warm: Scouts came back hungry and wouldn't shut up — {place.name}, {place.name}, until the rest of us had the word too.

voice.mythic: {place.name}. We have added it to what we know. The knowing is small yet.
voice.warm: {place.name} — one more thing we know about this country. Not much yet, but it's more than yesterday.

soul.question: Someone tried a thing on purpose, and the world answered. That is new.

voice.mythic: A woman pressed seed into the mud to see what it would do. The mud answered. We know a new thing.
voice.warm: One of the women pushed a seed into the mud just to see. The mud answered her. That's something we know now that we didn't.

voice.mythic: We have learned that a thing put in the ground comes back to the same place. It sounds small. It is not small.
voice.warm: Turns out if you put something in the ground, it comes back — and it comes back where you left it. Sounds like nothing. It isn't nothing.

voice.mythic: The {ground.adjective} ground has always fed us by accident. This season we made it do so on purpose.
voice.warm: The {ground.adjective} ground's always fed us by luck. This year we made it do it on purpose.

soul.question: A beast that does not run from us is a different kind of beast.

voice.mythic: The {beast.plural} did not run this time. They watched us, and stayed, and we understood that something had changed in them — or in us.
voice.warm: The {beast.plural} didn't run this time. They just watched us and stayed put. Something's changed in them. Or in us.

voice.mythic: We have stopped catching {beast.plural} and started keeping them. It is a smaller feast and a longer one.
voice.warm: We've stopped catching {beast.plural} and started keeping them instead. Less meat today. More of it for longer.

soul.question: We took what was there, and now it is not there. Whether that was a choice is the question we avoid.

voice.mythic: The {beast.plural} are thin on the ground this season, and thinner where we hunted hardest. The old ones have started counting aloud.
voice.warm: There aren't many {beast.plural} left out there, and fewest where we hunted hardest. The old ones have started counting them out loud, which is never a good sign.

voice.mythic: We found no young {beast.plural} at all this year. Only the old, and not many of those.
voice.warm: Didn't see a single young {beast.name} this year. Just old ones, and not many of them either.

voice.mythic: Where the {beast.plural} were loud enough to wake us, it is quiet now. We did that. We are not saying it aloud.
voice.warm: The {beast.plural} used to wake us up out there. It's quiet now. That was us, and nobody's saying so.

voice.mythic: The chase is longer every season and ends in less. The {beast.plural} were the road we walked; the road is going quiet under us.
voice.warm: Every season the chase runs longer and comes back with less. The {beast.plural} were how we got anywhere. That road's going quiet.

voice.mythic: The {beast.plural} thin out past the fields, and fewer of us look up when they pass. There is less reason to follow anything now.
voice.warm: The {beast.plural} are thinning out past the fields, and fewer of us bother watching them go. Less reason to follow anything these days.

soul.question: We have been in one place long enough that the young no longer ask when we leave. Is that who we are?

voice.mythic: The river-bend remembers us now. The young no longer ask when we leave.
voice.warm: We've been at the river-bend long enough that it knows us. The young ones have stopped asking when we move on.

voice.mythic: There are paths here now, worn by our own feet, going to places only we go. That is how a country becomes a home, or a trap.
voice.warm: There are proper paths here now, worn in by us, running to places only we bother going. That's how somewhere turns into home. Or a trap.

voice.mythic: We have buried our dead in this ground. It is harder to walk away from ground that holds them.
voice.warm: We've buried people in this ground now. Makes it harder to just walk away from it.

voice.mythic: Three seasons, and each one we chased the {beast.plural} and left the seed-ground unturned. The children do not remember a walled night. At the fires, they have begun to call us the People of the Long Chase. Is that who we are?
voice.warm: Three seasons now, all of them spent following the {beast.plural}, and nobody's turned the seed-ground once. The children have never slept behind a wall. People have started calling us the People of the Long Chase. Is that us?

echo.mythic: So it is said, and so we are: the People of the Long Chase.
echo.warm: So that's us, then. People of the Long Chase.

label.warm: No — we were meant to settle

echo.mythic: Let them call us what they like. We were meant to root, and here is the ground.
echo.warm: Let them call us whatever. We were meant to settle, and this is the ground for it.

echo.mythic: The question was not answered. The fires keep it.
echo.warm: Nobody answered. The fires keep the question.

soul.question: There is less than there was, and everyone has noticed.

voice.mythic: The children's ribs are counting themselves. No one says so at the fire.
voice.warm: You can count the children's ribs. Nobody mentions it at the fire.

voice.mythic: The portions grew smaller without anyone deciding it. That is how it always begins.
voice.warm: Portions have been getting smaller and nobody actually decided that. That's usually how it starts.

soul.question: There are more of us than the old ground could have carried.

voice.mythic: We are more now than we were when we left. The old ground could not have carried this many. Say that to the ones who doubted the walking.
voice.warm: There are more of us now than when we set out. The old ground couldn't have fed this many — worth saying to the ones who didn't want to leave.

voice.mythic: There are names in this camp that none of the old ones know. This is what surviving looks like from inside.
voice.warm: There are people in this camp the old ones can't name. That's what it looks like to be doing all right.
